ops.title: For Yasyf's own fleet infrastructure, prefer a comprehensive from-scratch redesign over a compat-preserving retrofit — no backwards compat, no versioning ceremony; break and migrate everything manually
ops.body:
During the 2026-07-21 cc-guides work, a plan that carefully preserved the old CI shape ("additive only, old consumers keep working, move the action-v1 tag") was rejected: *"no lets do a comprehensive redesign to a system that actually makes sense, given all we've learned, build it from scratch, and dont worry about backwards compat or versioning, just break and migrate everything manually."* The earlier turn of the same conversation set the direction the plan had under-delivered on: consumers should be dumb shims pointing at one shared, published brain, with all logic living upstream.

**Why:** the fleet is single-owner — every consumer can be migrated in one sweep, so compat layers, moving version tags, and transition shims are pure cost. Complexity that exists to serve a workflow being deleted (local rendering → version-skew guards, drift checks, per-consumer routing) should be deleted with it, not preserved.

**How to apply:** when a redesign of fleet-owned infrastructure (cc-guides, capt-hook, ccx, release pipelines) is on the table, default to the clean-slate architecture plus a manual full-fleet migration, and delete the machinery the old workflow required. Keep transition safety only when it's free (a frozen tag that old consumers resolve mid-sweep costs nothing). Versioned compat matters only for genuinely third-party surfaces. Related: [[cc-guides-v3-architecture]], [[never-sweep-guides-fleet]], [[fix-framework-not-hooks]].
